title: hieracium bupleuroides subsp. romanorum
intro: Hieracium bupleuroides subsp. romanorum, also known as Roman Hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds and skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The plant is also believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and acting as an antioxidant. In herbal preparations, Roman Hawkweed is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, and it is also sometimes combined with other herbs to enhance its effects.
